message:
fix(messaging): improve lock hygiene, correct module feature gate, and gate kafka tests

Three related fixes in the messaging subsystem:

1. Lock hygiene (kafka.rs, kafka_consumer.rs): add explicit drop(state)
   calls after MutexGuard usage to release parking_lot locks before
   subsequent operations. In kafka_consumer.rs commit_offsets(), extract
   subscribed_topics from the lock scope and validate outside the
   critical section, preventing potential deadlock under contention.
   Also drops the lock in unsubscribe(), seek(), and next_stub_offset().

2. Module feature gate (lib.rs): change pub mod messaging from
   #[cfg(all(not(target_arch = "wasm32"), feature = "kafka"))] to
   #[cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))]. The messaging module contains
   general-purpose infrastructure beyond Kafka; gating the entire module
   on the kafka feature was overly restrictive and prevented non-Kafka
   messaging types from being used.

3. Test feature gates (e2e_messaging.rs): add #[cfg(feature = "kafka")]
   to kafka-specific imports and test functions so the test file compiles
   without the kafka feature, consistent with the lib.rs gate change.

Also adds Eq derive to ConsumerRecord (already had PartialEq).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
